title: Career Direction Reset

problem.original: I feel stuck in my career and do not know what to improve first.
problem.understood: The user is not only looking for a better job. The immediate bottleneck is unclear direction, weak proof of skill, and no small feedback loop.
problem.bottleneck: The user lacks a visible next move that can produce feedback within one week.

outcome.desired: Choose a credible direction and create proof that can support better career options.
successSignals: A chosen target role or skill track

reverseEngineering.endState: The user can explain what they are pursuing, show evidence of progress, and ask for useful opportunities.
reverseEngineering.currentState: The user feels unfocused and is not converting effort into visible proof.
leveragePoints: Pick one role hypothesis for 30 days
Ask for feedback from three people

resources: Access to AI tools for planning and review
constraints: Avoid changing every variable at once

subgoals.title: Choose one career track hypothesis
subgoals.description: Select one direction to test for 30 days.

subgoals.title: Create one visible proof artifact
subgoals.description: Make a small project, case study, or writing sample that demonstrates the chosen track.

actions.title: List existing skills and proof
actions.description: Write down past work, strengths, and evidence that already exists.

actions.title: Pick one target role hypothesis
actions.description: Choose one role to test instead of keeping all possible paths open.

actions.title: Ship a small public proof artifact
actions.description: Publish one small artifact that makes the chosen direction visible.

milestones.title: First proof shipped
milestones.description: The user has something visible to discuss and improve.

milestones.title: 30-day direction review
milestones.description: The user decides whether to continue, adjust, or abandon the chosen track.

risks.title: Overplanning instead of creating proof
risks.mitigation: Limit planning to one hour, then create a visible artifact.

risks.title: Testing too many career directions at once
risks.mitigation: Use a 30-day track hypothesis instead of a permanent decision.

antiGoals: Do not redesign the whole life plan in one sitting.
Do not wait for perfect certainty before testing one direction.
Do not use AI output as final truth without personal review.

decisionPoints.question: After 30 days, did this track produce energy, evidence, or useful feedback?

supportPractices.label: Two-minute intention check
supportPractices.reason: This supports attention and commitment without replacing the practical action plan.
